observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to complete its physical inventory taking, render a report thereon and update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the PPE accounts totaling ₱324.519 million are presented fairly in the Statement of Financial Position as of December 31, 2023. Henceforth, periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken and regarded with utmost importance.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year's report. (Observation No. 2)

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer both agreed to facilitate the return of the unexpended balances of fund transfers to Source Agencies after confirming that the purpose of the fund transfers had been fulfilled.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report. (Observation Number 3)

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Department Heads concerned agreed to: (a) inventory all documents in their offices to determine valueless records due for disposal; and (b) prepare the request for disposal and, upon approval, submit the same to the National Archives of the Philippines (NAP) in accordance with prescribed guidelines.
observations.reason: No request for the disposal of records had been furnished. Department heads are keeping current in-use documents and or records in their offices.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately
record all the reconciling items preferably in the year of incurrence or upon
completion of updated BRS to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank
account in the financial statements as of year-end pursuant to COA Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

2.10. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Chairperson of the
Inventory Committee, under the supervision of the Municipal Treasurer,
agreed to reconcile records and undertake collaborative procedures to
ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their
respective records to fairly present the PPE accounts in the financial
statement.

3.7. We recommended the following and management agreed that:
a.) Concerned offices designate a Personnel-In-Charge to monitor the status of
implementation for each project; prepare the detailed summary
reports, including the fund utilization and remaining balances; and submit
reports to the Municipal Accountant promptly to ensure proper recording
in the books.
b.) The Municipal Accountant conducts a thorough review of expenditures;
prepares the utilization report to confirm remaining funds; and processes
to return unused balances of all completed projects to the respective
Source Agencies pursuant to the existing regulations.

4.10. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a) Inform in writing the concerned suppliers about their outstanding claims
with the advice that the amount will be reverted to the government equity
of the general fund when no claim is made within a reasonable period;
and
b) Record the reversion to the government equity of all accounts payable
that remained outstanding in the books for more than two years and not
supported by supporting documents.

5.8. We recommended and the Local School Board, thru the focal person, agreed
to submit the quarterly and annual SEF Utilization Reports within the period
required to the DepEd Central Office to ensure transparency and
accountability. We further recommend the establishment of a monitoring
system to track the timely preparation and submission of reports to concerned
offices within the prescribed period in adherence to existing rules and
regulations.

6.11. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the local
HRMO to formulate a uniform list of procedures in the recruitment of JO
workers to ensure that the hiring process is free from bias, including the
assigning of wage rates. A separate list of procedures shall also be created
for the evaluation of the accomplishments of JO workers for a certain period
to ensure that performance is commensurate with pay.

6.12. We further recommended and management also agreed that the assessment
and evaluation of the existing manpower complement to fill up vacant
plantilla positions be conducted in order to minimize the hiring of JO
employees.

8.5. We recommended and the management agreed to continue with the optimal
utilization of the 20% Development Fund including the proper management
of funds for proposed projects to give more benefit to constituents and
increase fund utilization.

11.4. We recommended and the local officials agreed to maintain effective control
mechanisms for PS expenditures and to continuously adhere to established
rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to send notices to the payees of the existence of their staled checks, accordingly cancel the staled checks, and forwards information with the supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant. Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of documents pertaining to the cancelled staled checks from the Municipal Treasurer, prepare the necessary entries in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary reversion of the outstanding balance of the BGCM in the accounting books to fairly present the affected account in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Inventory Committee, spearheaded by the Property Custodian, to undertake the required physical inventory of the municipality’s PPEs and submit to the Auditor an updated RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year. The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian based on the submitted RPCPPE shall update their records and resolve the discrepancies between the accounting and property records to come up with reliable PPE account balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the Implementing Offices to prepare and submit the Fund Utilization Reports on the Fund Transfers or grants received indicating the disbursements incurred in the project implementation or upon fulfilment of the purpose. Further, any unutilized amount shall be reported and returned to the Source Agencies and appropriate disclosure shall be made in the consolidated Notes to FS at year-end for the outstanding balance.

We recommended and the LGU agreed that the designated Municipal Property Custodian shall file an application for unserviceable assets disposal as provided for by existing regulations, and the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of the unserviceable items to Other Assets Account during the disposal process and upon disposal, drop them from the book of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Inventory Committee, spearheaded by the Property Custodian, to undertake the required physical inventory of the municipality’s PPEs and submit to the Auditor an updated RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year. The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian based on the submitted RPCPPE shall update their records and resolve the discrepancies between the accounting and property records to come up with reliable PPE account balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary reversion of the outstanding balance of the BGCM in the accounting books to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to send notices to the payees of the existence of their stale checks, cancel stale checks and forwards information with the supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant. Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of documents pertaining to the cancelled stale checks from the Municipal Treasurer, to prepare the necessary entries in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations.

We recommended and the LGU agreed that the designated LGU Municipal Property Custodian shall file an application for various unserviceable properties disposal of the properties as provided for by existing unserviceable regulations and the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of the unserviceable items to Other Assets Account during the disposal process and upon disposal drop them from the book of accounts.

We recommended and the Department Heads concerned agreed to: (a) inventory all documents in their offices to determine valueless records due for disposal; and (b) prepare the request for disposal and upon approval submit the same to the National Archives of the Philippines (NAP) in accordance with prescribed guidelines.

1.8. We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to complete its
physical inventory taking, render a report thereon and update and reconcile
the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the PPE accounts
totaling ₱324.519 million are presented fairly in the Statement of Financial
Position as of December 31, 2023.
1.9. Henceforth, periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of related
subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken and regarded with
utmost importance.

2.7. We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to send notices to
the payees of the existence of their staled checks, accordingly cancel the
staled checks, and forwards information with the supporting documents
to the Municipal Accountant. Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant,
upon receipt of documents pertaining to the cancelled staled checks from
the Municipal Treasurer, prepare the necessary entries in accordance
with prescribed rules and regulations.

3.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer both agreed to facilitate the return of the unexpended balances
of fund transfers to Source Agencies after confirming that the purpose of
the fund transfers had been fulfilled.

4.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary reversion of the
outstanding balance of the BGCM in the accounting books to fairly
present the affected account in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the current Municipal Treasurer agreed to review the Cash in Treasury account for all funds and reflect the necessary adjustment to correct both balances to present fairly said account in the financial statements; We also recommended that the Cash in Treasury balance of the previous MT shall be recorded as Other Receivable to reflect a reliable Cash in Local Treasury of the incumbent MT.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the Implementing Offices to prepare and submit the Fund Utilization Reports on the Fund Transfers or grants received indicating the disbursements incurred in the project implementation or upon fulfilment of the purpose. Further, any unutilized amount shall be reported and returned to the Source Agencies and appropriate disclosure shall be made in the consolidated Notes to FS at year-end for the outstanding balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other Implementing Offices determine the status of these projects, whether they are still implementable or not. Otherwise, the MPDC and other concerned offices recommend to the Municipal Development Council (MDC) for the realignment of the fund to other priority development projects and programs under the 20% DF. We also recommended that thereafter the MDC include priority development projects that are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those which are essential to the promotion of the general welfare in the 20% DF Plan and these projects must be well planned, implementation-and-procurement ready. We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to coordinate with the MPDC regarding projects funded by the 20% DF, especially those that remained unobligated, and, for projects no longer feasible, make representation to the Municipal Mayor to realign the projects to other priority development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of the Report on Utilization of LDRRMF in the prescribed format; and We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the financial information related to LDRRMF are appropriately taken up in the books and properly presented in the Financial Statements with adequate disclosures and shall facilitate the submission of said report to concerned offices within the prescribed period to comply with mentioned COA Circular.

We recommended and the Municipality officials agreed that through the GAD Focal Person the Annual GAD Accomplishment report shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from end of January of the preceding year together with the required supporting documents in compliance with the existing regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize the financial assistance of this kind as trust liability in the GF in consonance with regulations. Accordingly, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer properly appropriate the fund through budget legislation following the DBM circular.

We recommended and the Management Officials agreed that, through its LDRRM Council, will review and consolidate all the available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and incorporated in the AIP 2023. We also recommended and the Municipal Officials also agreed that the LDRRM Officer ensures the timely utilization of the fund to improve the Municipality’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended and the Municipal officials agreed that the Program Coordinator at the Municipal Health Office furnish the Municipal Accountant with the approved detailed list of allocated vaccines received and utilized by the Municipality, and to recognize in the books the donations in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor the completeness of the DVs from initial receipt until after recording in the journals in order to timely submit all DVs with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 73 checks issued totaling P6.702 million to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, Notices of Suspension for these disbursements will be issued.

We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in the SEF budget programs for health, nutrition, early education and social services for young children aged zero to four years to support the ECCD program of the government.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances amounting to P5,929,391.00 pursuant to existing regulations, otherwise require the persons accountable to refund the cash advances granted to them.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to refrain from granting additional cash advances to employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances, and to strictly comply with the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the same.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review the Cash in Treasury account and reflect the necessary adjustment to correct its balance to be presented fairly in the financial statements.
We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to update her cashbooks regularly and to reconcile them with the updated accounting records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately record all the reconciling items found in the bank reconciliation statements to report a reliable Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account balance at year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Inventory Committee, spearheaded by the Property Custodian, to undertake the required physical inventory of PPEs and submit to the Auditor an updated RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.  After which, appropriate adjustment shall be made by the Municipal Accountant on PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Personnel-In-charge in each concerned office monitor the status of implementation for each project; prepare the summary report including the determination of the remaining balances, and timely submit reports to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books; and
The Municipal Accountant, upon verification of reports, prepare the utilization report and return unused balances of all completed projects to the Source Agency pursuant to the existing regulations.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately return the unobligated balance of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and secure proof of the return thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize the financial assistance of this kind as trust liability in the GF in consonance with regulations. Accordingly, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer properly appropriate the fund through budget legislation following the DBM circular.

We recommended and the Management agreed that, through its LDRRM Council, the concerned Municipal officials will review and consolidate all available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of available funds.
We also recommended and the Municipal Officials also agreed that the LDRRM Officer ensures the timely utilization of the fund to improve the Municipality’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary reversion of the outstanding balance of the BGCM in the accounting books to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the recipient frontliners to submit the essential documents as proofs that they were eligible for the payment of ADHP to justify the validity, regularity and propriety of the payments.

We recommended and the Municipal officials agreed that the Program Coordinator at the Municipal Health Office furnish the Municipal Accountant with the approved detailed list of allocated vaccines received and utilized by the Municipality, and to recognize in the books the donations in accordance with existing regulations.
We recommended and the Municipal Health Officer agreed to exert efforts to promote vaccine confidence and encourage more of its populace to vaccinate in order to attain the herd immunity.

We recommended and the management agreed that reconciliation of records between the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be undertaken pursuant to Section 266, GAM, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.

Furthermore, we recommended and the same offices agreed to investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory was started to reconcile the difference.
observations.reason: Partial inventory only was performed and no updated and reconciled RPCPPE was submitted for CY 2021. Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to send notices to the payees of the existence of their stale checks, cancel stale checks and forwards information with the supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant. Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant also agreed, upon receipt of documents pertaining to the cancelled stale checks from the Municipal Treasurer, to prepare the necessary entries in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Some of the stale checks had already been adjusted.
observations.reason: On-going validation of all staled checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant immediately records in the specific bank accounts all the reconciling items found in the bank reconciliation statements to reflect the correct balances at year-end so that accuracy and reliability of the Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account can be validated.
observations.managementAction: Booked some reconciling items.
observations.reason: There were still reconciling items that require validation. Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed that the designated Municipal Property Custodian shall file an application for disposal of the unserviceable properties as provided for by existing regulations and the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of the unserviceable items to Other Assets Account during the disposal process and upon disposal drop them from the book of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Started on gathering of documents and information on the various unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: No request had been submitted for disposal of properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct periodic physical count of Biological Assets, prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA) in accordance with the guidelines above and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries to fairly present the amount in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Confirmed with personnel in charge.
observations.reason: The amount was still not adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements (OSSs) from the suppliers, and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases in case the supplier fails to submit complete documentary requirements after the contract has been awarded.
observations.managementAction: All items purchased were delivered.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor the completeness of the DVs from initial receipt until after recording in the journals in order to timely submit all DVs with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 73 checks issued totaling P6,702,611.46 to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, Notices of Suspension for these disbursements will be issued.
observations.managementAction: Some DVs were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review and update their existing SWM Plan to include the required components and submit the same to the National Commission for approval in compliance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Actual Plan was already prepared and submitted to EMB for approval.
Was given a provisional approval to implement programs embodied in the Plan.
observations.reason: Plan still under provisional approval. Lack of funds to construct the required facilities for Plan to be approved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LDRRM Council agreed to review and consolidate all available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of available funds.

We also recommended and the LDRRM Officer agreed to ensure the timely utilization of the fund to improve the Municipality’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.
observations.managementAction: Some plans were not implemented due to COVID19 constraints.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Department Heads concerned agreed to: inventory all documents in their offices to determine valueless records due for disposal; and prepare the request for disposal and upon approval submit the same to the National Archives of the Philippines (NAP) in accordance with prescribed guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Department heads are only keeping current and in use documents or records in their offices.
observations.reason: No request for disposal of records had been furnished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closure of the dormant accounts and cause the transfer of the balance totaling P585,108.16 to the Municipality’s active account or return immediately the unutilized amount and submit the Report of Disbursement or Liquidation Report to the source agencies for accounts under the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Accounts were validated for closure.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts still exist as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit to the Auditor an updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Inventory was done and RPCPEE was prepared for identified assets.
observations.reason: Overtaken by new regulations and inventory taking in CY 2021 was started.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause/s of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise prudence in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are at all times equal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had adjusted and reconciled the balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in the SEF budget programs for health, nutrition, early education and social services development for young children aged zero to four years to support the ECCD program of the government.
observations.managementAction: No information had been provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to process payments on infrastructure projects that are supported with the required documents, and in accordance with existing regulations.

Furthermore, lacking supporting documents on the identified transactions above should be submitted for evaluation to prevent from being suspended.

We recommended and the management agreed that reconciliation of records between the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be undertaken pursuant to Section 266, GAM, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.
Furthermore, we recommended and the same offices agreed to investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to send notices to the payees of the existence of their stale checks, cancel stale checks and forwards information with the supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant.  Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant also agreed, upon receipt of documents pertaining to the cancelled stale checks from the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the necessary entries in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant immediately records in the specific bank accounts all the reconciling items found in the bank reconciliation statements to reflect the correct balances at year-end so that accuracy and reliability of the Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account can be validated.

We recommended and the LGU agreed that the designated Municipal Property Custodian  shall file an application for disposal of the unserviceable properties as provided for by existing regulations and the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of the unserviceable items to Other Assets Account during the disposal process and upon disposal drop them from the book of accounts.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct periodic physical count of Biological Assets, prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA) in accordance with the guidelines above and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries to fairly present the amount in the financial statements.

We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements (OSSs) from the suppliers, and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases in case the supplier fails to submit complete documentary requirements after the contract has been awarded.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor the completeness of the DVs from initial receipt until after recording in the journals in order to timely submit all DVs with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 73 checks issued totaling P6,702,611.46 to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, Notices of Suspension for these disbursements will be issued.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review and update their existing SWM Plan to include the required components and submit the same to the National Commission for approval in compliance with existing regulations.

We recommended and the LDRRM Council agreed to review and consolidate all available LDRRM Funds, formulate viable projects and activities, and maximize the use of available funds.
We also recommended and the LDRRM Officer agreed to ensure the timely utilization of the fund to improve the Municipality’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended and the Department Heads concerned agreed to:
inventory all documents in their offices to determine valueless records due for disposal; and
prepare the request for disposal and upon approval submit the same to the National Archives of the Philippines (NAP) in accordance with prescribed guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the transfer of the remaining PNB account balances to any of the authorized government depository banks in the nearby Municipality of Mamburao or secure approval and/or clearance from the DOF authorizing PNB as the duly approved depository bank, if the Municipality still intends to continue with PNB’s services.
observations.reason: Management forgot and overlooked the transfer of all PNB account balances to the Land Bank account during the year, hence this finding is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closure of the dormant accounts and cause the transfer of the balance totaling P585,108.16 to the Municipality’s active account or return immediately the unutilized amount and submit the Report of Disbursement or Liquidation Report to the source agencies for accounts under the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit to the Auditor an updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause/s of the unreconciled amounts.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise prudence in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are at all times equal.

We recommended that the concerned personnel submit the lacking document to support claim for monetization of leave credits pursuant to existing regulations.

We further recommended that management strictly observe the existing CSC rules and regulations and Section 5.14 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in processing application of monetization of leave credits of its officials and employees.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the processing and granting of applications for terminal leave benefits to its officials and employees.

Likewise, please submit the lacking documents mentioned above to enable us to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that required documentary requirements are duly attached to the disbursement vouchers before approving any release of transfers to various barangays pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in the SEF budget programs for health, nutrition, early education and social services development for young children aged zero to four years to support the ECCD program of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require responsible officials in the monitoring of loans payable of the municipality, to submit a written explanation why they should not be liable to pay the penalty of P262,487.49 for the late payment of DOF loan. Henceforth, instruct them to strictly monitor loans re-payments schedule to avoid penalties in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor provide the Municipal Treasurer with the assessment roll as the basis for the Municipal Treasurer in collecting the real property tax. Accordingly, we also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers, as well as its total amount due and collectible for the year 2019 and thereafter so that RPT and SET Receivables is set-up and book at the beginning of every year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant allow the utilization of the 20% Development Fund strictly in accordance with the existing regulations, and utilize only for programs/projects/activities that are capital expenditure in nature to attain the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for its constituents.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to process payments on infrastructure projects that are supported with the required documents, and in accordance with existing regulations.  Furthermore, lacking supporting documents on the identified transactions above should be submitted for evaluation to prevent from being suspended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the transfer of
   the remaining PNB account to any of the authorized government  depository
   banks in the nearby Municipality of Mamburao or  secure  approval  and/or
   clearance from the DOF authorizing PNB as the  duly  approved  depository
   bank, if the Municipality still intends to continue with PNB’s services.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  continuously  demand
   these AOs for the immediate  settlement  of  their  accountabilities  and
   ensure that all cash advances are settled/liquidated at year-end.


        We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor  impose  sanctions  to
   these concerned accountable officers who failed to liquidate  their  cash
   advances on  the  prescribed  period,  and  if  warranted,  consider  the
   imposition of legal remedies to enforce settlement of their  accounts  as
   prescribed by Section 128 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
   Treasurer and the focal person of the loan programs to (i) conduct proper
   orientation to all loan proponents before the grant  of  their  loans  to
   instill in their minds the  value  of  responsibility;  (ii)  assess  the
   collection strategy, (iii) take possible steps/strategies to collect  the
   loan receivable accounts from borrowers to ensure cash  availability  for
   future beneficiaries of  various  loan  programs,  and  (iv)  exert  more
   efforts to collect those past due accounts. We also recommended that  the
   Municipal Mayor evaluate the effectiveness of the  program  to  serve  as
   guide in determining whether to continue the program or to suspend  until
   all the receivables are collected.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal  Mayor  require
   the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the physical count  of
   all Inventory and PPE and to prepare and submit the RPCI  and  RPCPPE  to
   the Office of the Audit Team on the period prescribed by Section  124  of
   the NGAS Manual, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant, to facilitate the
   reconciliation with accounting records. The  Municipal  Treasurer/General
   Services Officer is advised to maintain a complete and updated  perpetual
   inventory records which should always  reconcile  with  the  PPE  general
   ledger balances. Any discrepancy between these records should be properly
   examined to facilitate adjustment of the affected  PPE  account  balances
   for its fair and accurate presentation  in  the  Statement  of  Financial
   Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


     a. require the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation  of
        various projects funded  out  of  grants  from  different  National
        Government Agencies;

     b. request from the source agency/ies for  authority  to  use/transfer
        the unexpended Trust Fund balances to General Fund  or  direct  the
        Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended trust fund balances  to
        the Source Agencies, if the authority for  the  use  of  unexpended
        trust fund balances is not approved; and


     c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to i) investigate  the  cause  of
        the differences between the balance of Due to NGAs account  in  the
        FS and in the Schedule of Due to NGAs as of year-end; ii) reconcile
        both records; and iii) prepare the necessary adjustments to correct
        the difference.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


       a. Bids and Awards  Committee  (BAC)  to  strictly  comply  with  the
          provisions of  the  2016  Revised  IRR  of  RA  No.  9184  on  the
          procurement  of  goods,   particularly,   Annex   H   Consolidated
          Guidelines for the Alternative Methods of Procurement;


       b. Supplier to post the required performance  security  to  guarantee
          the faithful performance of its obligation under the contract  and
          warranty security in order to fully protect the  interest  of  the
          Municipality in case of manufacturing defects and failures;


       c. Municipal Accountant to  submit  the  duly  accomplished  JEV  and
          review the regularity and  completeness  of  supporting  documents
          prescribed under Sections 9.1 and 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 –
          001, before processing claims for payment;


       d. Property Officer to issue the necessary  Property  Acknowledgement
          Receipt (PAR) for those Property Plant and Equipment  (PPE)  under
          the care of  the  end  user  and  observe  the  use  of  Inventory
          Custodian Slip as provided by COA Circular No. 2005-002;


       e.  The  General  Services  Officer  (GSO)  and/or  office/department
          responsible for accepting deliveries  to  notify  and  invite  the
          Audit Team within 24 hours from acceptance of  all  deliveries  of
          goods, and inform the Audit Team of  the  time  and  date  of  the
          scheduled deliveries to avoid delay or failure in the  conduct  of
          inspection and other audit procedures necessary  pursuant  to  COA
          Circular No. 95-006; and


       f. BAC/GSO to observe the timely  submission  of  advance  copies  of
          contracts within  five  (5)  working  days  after  its  perfection
          pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


       a. Bids and Awards  Committee  (BAC)  to  strictly  comply  with  the
          provisions of  the  2016  Revised  IRR  of  RA  No.  9184  on  the
          procurement of Infrastructure Projects;


       b. Municipal Engineer  to  strictly  monitor  the  implementation  of
          contract to ensure compliance with Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR
          of  RA  No.  9184  and  with  the  full   disclosure   policy   by
          accomplishing the required information in the  signboard/tarpaulin
          as an integral part of supporting documents for claim of payment;


       c. Municipal Accountant to submit the duly accomplished JEV  together
          with  complete  and  duly   authenticated   supporting   documents
          prescribed under Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001; and


       d. BAC/GSO to observe the timely  submission  of  advance  copies  of
          contracts within  five  (5)  working  days  after  its  perfection
          pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


     a. Require the GFPS to  observe  the  schedule  of  GAD  Planning  and
        Budgeting as provided by Section 4.1.C.5 of  the  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
        JMC JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by Section  4  of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
        JMC No. 2016-01 dated  January  12,  2016,  to  ensure  the  timely
        preparation and submission of GPB with  reference  to  all  of  the
        observations of prior year’s GPB   cited  by  the  DILG  Provincial
        Office in their review; and


     b. Support and strengthen the GFPS by requiring her to attend  to  the
        appropriate capacity development program on gender and  development
        and to re-echo the same to all LGU’s employees as  part  of  the
        programs under its regular human resource  development  program  in
        compliance with Section 4.1.A.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA of JMC No.
        2013-01.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor,  thru  the  Municipal
   Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Office,  ensure
   the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur  development,
   provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired  socio-
   economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the  Municipal
   Accountant, enforce the settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances
   by requiring the persons responsible, therefore,  within  the  prescribed
   period as provided  under  Sections  7.1,  9.4  and  10.4,  of  the  RRSA
   prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory was done late in the CY 2018, however the required inventory report for CY 2018 was submitted only in August 2019.
observations.reason: Delayed conduct of physical count of all PPE and Inventory in prior year resulted in the late-submission of RPCI and RPCPPE for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: b) Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the PPE general ledger balances.  Any discrepancy between these records should be properly examined to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances for its fair and accurate presentation in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant: (a) send a demand letter to concerned AO’s to enforce liquidation of cash advance; (b) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that these are liquidated within the prescribed period; (c) prohibit/refrain from granting additional cash advances to those employees who still have unliquidated balances; (d) ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and (e) reconcile the financial statement account balance of P249,981.35 with the RUCA to ensure correctness.
observations.managementAction: The Management did their best to comply with the audit recommendations. Municipal Accountant sent demand letters to those AOs with outstanding balances and ensured that all previous cash advances are liquidated before the grant of another CA which resulted in their timely liquidation. As compared with prior year’s balance of unliquidated CA, it decreased by P124,937.90 or 47% and leaving only two (2) employees with unliquidated balances totaling P142,239.45 at year-end. The balances of RUCA and FS at CY2019 yearend were reconciled by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The two (2) accountable officers with a total balance of P142,239.45 which has been outstanding for three (3) to seven (7) years is still unmoving as at year-end of CY 2019. The former storekeeper let the withholding of his terminal leave benefits, however processing of his claims is still in progress as of this date and whether it would satisfy his obligations could not be determined, thus, his outstanding balance remains at year-end. This finding is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned departments to: assess the collection efficiency, take possible steps/strategies to collect the loan receivable from borrowers to ensure cash availability for future beneficiaries of various loan programs, and exert more efforts to collect those past due accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the effectiveness of the program to serve as guide in determining whether to continue the program or to suspend until all the receivables are collected.
observations.managementAction: The Management together with the focal persons of these loan programs conducted a meeting with the loan recipients and/or their authorized representative and reminded them of their responsibilities to pay the loans due. Verbal demand was made by the focal person, and the Municipal Accountant sent demand letters to those borrowers. During the year the Municipality collected the amount of P169,459.00.
observations.reason: Minimal amount was collected from the borrowers. Out of 130 borrowers, only 40 made partial payments, while the other 90 borrowers did not pay even a single centavo from their account balances due to lack of available cash.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the validity of the unliquidated obligations or accounts payable which were outstanding for more than two (2) years to five (5) years and cause the reversion of these accounts to the unappropriated surplus, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant already reverted those accounts which were outstanding for more than 2 years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Municipal Engineer to implement promptly the projects funded by the NGAs such as provision of water supply and core shelter housing projects. The Municipal Accountant to evaluate the need for those unexpended fund balances from NGAs. If still needed, Management should request for authority from the source agency to use the money for other related projects of the Municipality, otherwise, it should be returned to the source agencies as required under existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented projects funded by the NGAs such as provision of water supply and core shelter housing projects. Management committed that the unexpended balances from completed projects will be remitted to source agencies.
observations.reason: Slow project implementation due to procurement process, attributable to lack of interested qualified/available contractors to the projects to be bid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: Bids and Awards Committee to (i) strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of goods through public bidding and other modes of procurement and ensure strict adherence of the conditions/requirements under Annex H Consolidated Guidelines for the Alternative Methods of Procurement; and (ii) demand from the Supplier the posting of warranty security in order to fully protect the interest of the Municipality; Municipal Accountant to review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.1 and 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001, before processing claims for payment and submit the duly accomplished JEV; and BAC/GSO to observe the timely submission of advance copies of contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) working days after its perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the lacking documents on the prior projects with deficiencies and became more cautious in the procurement procedure.
observations.reason: Procedural lapses still occurred in the procurement process. Bulk of transactions in the Office of the Municipal Accountant resulted in the failure of ensuring the completeness of necessary supporting documents. BAC/GSO still failed to submit the approved contracts/PO within 5 days after its perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: BAC and/or GSO to observe timely submission of the advance copies of contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001; Municipal Engineer to (a) strictly monitor implementation of the contract to ensure compliance with Annexes E and G of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (b) ensure that the contractor submit the As Built Plans and Drawings before processing final payment; and (c) ensure that the contractor comply with the full disclosure policy by taking photographs of signboard as part of the supporting documents for claim of payment; Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly scrutinize all transactions to avoid incurrence of erroneous computations; and (b) make the necessary adjustments for the underpayment to correct the balances of affected accounts in the financial statements; Municipal Treasurer to cause the refund to the contractor due to double recoupment of the 15% Mobilization.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the lacking documents on the prior projects with deficiencies and became more cautious in the procurement procedure. Municipal Engineer strictly monitored the project implementation and the photos of billboards for every project as part of the supporting documents were now attached. Adjusted the balance of Advance payment to the contractor and the Cash in Bank account for the refund of mobilization due to double recoupment or underpayment to the contractor. Refunded the amount of P542,379.42 to the contractor for the over recoupment of Mobilization on the progress payments.
observations.reason: Procedural lapses still occurred in the procurement process. Bulk of transactions in the Office of the Municipal Accountant resulted in the failure of ensuring the completeness of necessary supporting documents. BAC/GSO still failed to submit the approved contracts/PO within 5 days after its perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to: (a) use 5% VAT in computing the ABC pursuant to the DPWH Department Order No. 22 as amended by DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2016; (b) closely monitor/supervise project implementation to ensure contractor’s compliance with the plan and technical specifications of the contract; and (c) compute the liquidated damages on project with delayed completion.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer now used the 5% VAT component in preparing the Program of Works and Approved Budget for the Contract; the liquidated damages were computed on projects with delayed completion, while the cost deficiencies were already deducted from the payment made to the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the MPDO and Municipal Engineer maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDO to: (i) faithfully carry out the developmental objectives for which the 20% Development Fund was established; and (ii) diligently prepare the plans and design of priority projects before the same are included in the AIP to facilitate the implementation; MPDO and the Municipal Engineer to conduct regular monitoring on the planned investment projects and evaluate its implementation to avoid delays; and MPDO and Municipal Engineer to observe other related actions.
observations.managementAction: Municipality completed 28 developmental projects and 34 projects are on-going as of year-end. It only registered at 26.10% Financial Delivery Rate, since most of the claims are still in process at year-end.
observations.reason: Forty-eight (48) projects in current and continuing appropriations were not implemented due to voluminous transactions and prolonged procurement process as failure of biddings always occurred since only limited contractors are willing to participate in the competitive bidding process. Issues such as Road right of way, project location which are in forest land and its accessibility also contributed in the delay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to: (a) maximize the utilization of the 70% LDRRMF for the programs, projects and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation in order to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to calamities and climate change impacts; and (b) procure the basic equipment and other equipage needed to effectively respond to imminent or current hazard events or conditions and ensure public safety towards meeting the basic needs of the constituents in case of catastrophes.
observations.managementAction: Municipality utilized P14,589,634.53 out of the P15,850,122.44 of the total 70% Mitigation Fund component of the LDRRM Fund registering a 92.05% financial delivery rate which exceeded the minimum requirement of the DILG for the Utilization of 70% Mitigation Fund, thus, made them qualified for the SGLG. PPAs in their LDRRM Plan were implemented/procured. Moreover, the amount of P217,190.46 or 7.97% was utilized for post disaster/calamity activities from the 30% Quick Response Fund (QRF).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) enforce the settlement of suspensions and disallowances by requiring, the persons responsible and/or liable to settle their accountabilities within the prescribed period as provided under Section 9.4 and 10.4 of the COA Circular No. 2009-006; and (b) submit the official receipts and/or other necessary documents as proof of payments/settlement of disallowances to enable the issuance of NSSDC.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Mayor settled the ND No.18-001-101-(16)-17 amounting to P137,700.00, which pertains to the transportation allowance of the former Municipal Mayor. Partial payments amounting to P102,400.00, P1,600.00 and P418,935.00 were made by the responsible persons to settle the NDs amounting to P128,000.00, P6,600.00 and P571,420.86 respectively.
observations.reason: Partial payments amounting to P102,400.00, P1,600.00 and P418,935.00 were made by the responsible persons to settle the NDs amounting to P128,000.00, P6,600.00 and P571,420.86 respectively. However, since they failed to request for the installment settlement of their ND from the COA Regional Office, no NSSDS for partial settlement for this partial payment was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the required documentary requirements are duly attached to the disbursement vouchers before approving any release of funds to various barangays pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipality direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and Property Custodian records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor of Ilog must secure approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian for those projects/programs/activities described in generic terms or for all those lump-sum appropriation prior to its implementation pursuant to Supreme Court En Banc Decision G.R. No. 175527 dated December 8, 2008 and reiterated in COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 dated April 22, 2010.

We recommended that the Municipality strengthen the implementation of its identified PPAs under LDRRM Plan to better prepare for any calamity and to enhance the municipality’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all advances to Officers and Employees and Special Disbursing Officer are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineers Office submit regularly to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the Accomplishment Report on Infrastructure Projects and copies of Certificate of Completion and Acceptance to be used as basis for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the transfer to appropriate PPE – Public Infrastructure accounts.
We further recommended that the Municipality adhere to the provisions of Section 50 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I to enable close monitoring of the projects still in progress and to make necessary adjusting entries to transfer the identified completed projects to avoid overstatement of the ‘Construction in Progress” accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects so that the optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund/remit the excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to avoid possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that management should adhere strictly to the general provisions of the GAA and ensure that their salaries are protected from further deductions which will reduce their net take home pay.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 for those granted before December 31, 2011.

The Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers to strictly comply with the guidelines provided in Items 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 in the liquidation of their cash advances.

The Municipal Mayor likewise monitor the liquidation and demand settlement if no liquidation was submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason:
Finding reiterated
in this year’s
report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that programs/projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund should be utilized immediately considering that these are priority projects to provide for  the immediate needs of the constituents of the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon within the prescribed deadline pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Training Handbook on Property & Supply Management System, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and General Services Office records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit disbursement vouchers, official receipts and supporting documents due for submission to the Audit Team.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the deadline on the submission of aforementioned documents pursuant to Section 7.2.1 a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 100 and 107 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly cause the remittance in full the amount withheld to the BIR within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding accrued. This practice shall be constantly enforced.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor maximize the unutilization of the LDRRM Fund as of December 31, 2017, to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disaster and climate change impacts.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should allocate fund for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children, pursuant to Section 15 of RA 9344.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the GAD funds be fully utilized and maximized for GAD programs and activities only.

We recommended that the agency require the accountable officers concerned to immediately submit liquidation documents to settle their overdue cash advances and abide with the regulations set forth under Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit disbursement vouchers, official receipts and supporting documents for transactions covering the period January to December 2016, pursuant to Section 100 and 107 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 on the rendering of accounts by accountable officers.
Henceforth, timing of submission as required under cited regulations should be strictly complied.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from granting fund transfer from DILG for Payapa at Masaganang Pamayanan of PAMANA-DILG Fund for Infrastructure Component without documentary requirements contrary to DILG-Joint Memorandum Circular No. 3, Series of 2016.

The Municipal Treasurer should reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records every end of the month to make sure that the balances appearing in the cashbook are correct.
In the same manner, the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the reports of the Municipal Treasurer should verify the correctness of the report and shall reconcile her GL/SL accounts with the cashbook every end of the month for a sound internal control.

We recommended that management should adhere strictly to the general provisions of the GAA and ensure that their salaries are protected from further deductions which will reduce their net take home pay.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 for those granted before December 31, 2011.
The Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers to strictly comply with the guidelines provided in Items 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 in the liquidation of their cash advances.
The Municipal Mayor likewise monitor the liquidation and demand settlement if no liquidation was submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommended that programs/projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund  should be utilized immediately considering that these are priority projects to provide for  the immediate needs of the constituents of the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon within the prescribed deadline pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Training Handbook on Property & Supply Management System, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and General Services Office records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit	 disbursement vouchers, official receipts and supporting documents due for submission to the Audit Team.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the deadline on the submission of aforementioned documents pursuant to Section 7.2.1 a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 100 and 107 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly cause the remittance in full the amount withheld to the BIR within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding accrued. This practice shall be constantly enforced.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor maximize the unutilization of the LDRRM Fund as of December 31, 2017, to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disaster and climate change impacts.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should allocate fund for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children, pursuant to Section 15 of RA 9344.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the GAD funds be fully utilized and maximized for GAD programs and activities only.

We recommended that the agency require the accountable officers concerned to immediately submit liquidation documents to settle their overdue cash advances and abide with the regulations set forth under Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit disbursement vouchers, official receipts and supporting documents for transactions covering the period January to December 2016, pursuant to Section 100 and 107 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 on the rendering of accounts by accountable officers. Henceforth, timing of submission as required under cited regulations should be strictly complied.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit the CY 2016 GAD fund accomplishment report, providing documentation on Gender and Development Programs/Projects, pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-01 dated April 5, 2004 issued by the DBM, NEDA, and Philippine Commission on Women.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from granting fund transfer from DILG for Payapa at Masaganang Pamayanan of PAMANA-DILG Fund for Infrastructure Component without documentary requirements contrary to DILG-Joint Memorandum Circular No. 3, Series of 2016.

The Municipal Treasurer should reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records every end of the month to make sure that the balances appearing in the cashbook are correct. In the same manner, the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the reports of the Municipal Treasurer should verify the correctness of the report and shall reconcile her GL/SL accounts with the cashbook every end of the month for a sound internal control.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply strictly with the above cited regulations for us to monitor properly and account all the expenditures properly to assure that government funds are well expended.

observations.managementAction: The management complied on the COA Circular 97-002 in granting of cash advances. Most of the employees/offi cial in the list already liquidated their cash advances but there were still unliquidated advances at year-end.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management to remit promptly the taxes withheld from the employees to the BIR and monthly premiums, contributions and loan repayments to the GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth in the total amount of P1,890,046.27, as prescribed under Section 6 of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997 and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17 1998 and Section 6 (a) of PD 1146.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned offices be directed to submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption per vehicle supported with approved and properly accomplished trip tickets, monthly report of travels undertaken, daily gas issue record, and fuel control card or logbook.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should create and Inventory Committee and require them to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Physical count of inventory was conducted in 2017 and a report of the result of the inventory was made.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately prepare and submit to COA the Monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability of Accountable Forms (CRAAF) for CY 2015 and every month thereafter duly supported with the RAAFs, RCDs and ORs to facilitate the determination of the accountability of all collecting officers and of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the depository accounts of the agency in order to determine the correctness of the cash-in-bank appearing in the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant should submit the statements to the Auditor for verification in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records every end of the month to make sure that the balances appearing in the cashbook are correct. In the same manner, the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the reports of the Municipal Treasurer should verify the correctness of the report and shall reconcile her GL/SL accounts with the cashbook every end of the month for a sound internal control.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the MDRRMO adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the audit team concerned the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and the Local Development Council.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendations.
